summary: Verified bug fix for Evaluaciones cuestionario NOT saving 'No' (false) answers. Frontend fix (`||` → `??`) in /app/frontend/src/pages/Evaluaciones.js line 274 works correctly end-to-end. Backend accepts and persists boolean false; PDF now renders 'R: No' for question 9; UI reload shows the No button still highlighted in red after save.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correct — nullish coalescing (`??`) properly preserves booleans and 0 for si_no/numero types, while keeping empty-string default for texto/fecha. Backend already handled the payload correctly (validated via curl in prior iteration).

Consider adding data-testid attributes to the Sí/No buttons of each si_no question (e.g. `question-{id}-yes`, `question-{id}-no`) to make future automated testing more robust — currently they can only be located by text/order.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing evaluación id 6a3e4b01e223c5dd1673c04c (COTO DE MINGUILLO / GUISANTE VERDE).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The evaluación 6a3e4b01e223c5dd1673c04c has been modified during testing to have td_9=False, td_1=True, td_5='Limpios'. If you retest, know that these values are the current baseline in DB. The frontend UI persists boolean false correctly now.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Evaluaciones.js the handleSubmit built the payload with `respuesta: respuestas[p.id] || ''`. Since JavaScript treats `false` (No) as falsy, the `||` operator replaced it with empty string `''`, so the boolean answer never reached the backend. The stored respuesta was a string, which caused the PDF (which expects boolean true/false to print 'Sí'/'No') to print an empty response. FIX: Changed `||` to nullish coalescing `??` — this only falls back to `''` when the value is null/undefined, preserving `false` and `0`. REPRODUCTION (before fix): click No on Q9, save, reload → button no longer red; GET returns respuesta:''; PDF shows blank. VALIDATION (after fix): backend pytest passes (4/4), full UI flow (edit → click No → save → reload → still red) works, PDF shows 'R: No' for Q9.
